notes:
dở means bad or not good in quality. You use it to describe things like movies, food, books, performances, ideas, etc.

It goes after the noun: phim dở, món ăn dở, bài hát dở. It can also describe someone’s ability: dở Toán = bad at math.

A very common opposite is hay (good/interesting). Stronger negative options are tệ or tồi.

It can also mean “unfinished” in some set phrases like dở dang (half-done), but alone it usually just means “bad.”
exampleSentences: Anh thấy bộ phim tối qua thật sự dở nên anh không muốn xem lại.
Em nấu món ăn này hơi dở, lần sau em sẽ cố gắng hơn.
Quyển sách này khá dở nên chị không đọc tiếp nữa.
exampleSentencesTranslated: I think the movie last night was really bad so I don't want to watch it again.
I cooked this dish a bit bad, next time I will try harder.
This book is quite bad so I won't keep reading it.
notes_md:
**dở** means *bad* or *not good* in quality. You use it to describe things like movies, food, books, performances, ideas, etc.

It goes after the noun: *phim dở, món ăn dở, bài hát dở*. It can also describe someone’s ability: *dở Toán* = bad at math.

A very common opposite is **hay** (good/interesting). Stronger negative options are **tệ** or **tồi**.

It can also mean “unfinished” in some set phrases like **dở dang** (half-done), but alone it usually just means “bad.”
